[x] ~~google.com~~ - [ ] instagram.com - [ ] threads.net - [ ] monkeytype.com - [ ] quora.com - [ ] search.brave.com - [ ] facebook.com - [ ] messenger.com - [ ] x.com ## Dynamic Transparency [Permalink: Dynamic Transparency](https://github.com/frostybiscuit/transparent-zen#dynamic-transparency) Transparent Zen has the option to make any website transparent by crawling the website and setting styles as good as possible. While this works well on many websites, this is a very early state of this feature and will be improved with future updates to get a better experience on more websites. For the case that a website is rendered unusable with this, it is possible to disable this per domain in the extension popup. ![image](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/128132341/433842580-bb86f9d3-2fe8-4143-a86e-df917babe59d.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJnaXRodWIuY29tIiwiYXVkIjoicmF3LmdpdGh1YnVzZXJjb250ZW50LmNvbSIsImtleSI6ImtleTUiLCJleHAiOjE3ODIzMjAwMTksIm5iZiI6MTc4MjMxOTcxOSwicGF0aCI6Ii8xMjgxMzIzNDEvNDMzODQyNTgwLWJiODZmOWQzLTJmZTgtNDE0My1hODZlLWRmOTE3YmFiZTU5ZC5wbmc_.aoLGrPcgKuYnPInZF7fMiSXTW0fym8OmWyvx96Uwi6I) #### To-Do [Permalink: To-Do](https://github.com/frostybiscuit/transparent-zen#to-do-1) - [x] ~~Reduce flashing on style application~~ - [x] Add per-site configuration - [x] ~~Add options to configure colors for supported sites~~ - [x] ~~Improve stability~~ - [ ] Find a solution for hover states - [x] ~~Rework popup for expandability~~ ## Prerequisites [Permalink: Prerequisites](https://github.com/frostybiscuit/transparent-zen#prerequisites) #### Windows 11 [Permalink: Windows 11](https://github.com/frostybiscuit/transparent-zen#windows-11) 1. Open Zen Browser and go to `about:config` 2. Make sure that `browser.tabs.allow_transparent_browser`, `widget.transparent-windows` and `widget.windows.mica` are all set to **true** 3. Install [MicaForEveryone](https://github.com/MicaForEveryone/MicaForEveryone) (optional) - In MicaForEveryone add a new process rule and select "zen" - Activate **Blur Behind** and set the Backdrop Type to **Acrylic** #### Linux [Permalink: Linux](https://github.com/frostybiscuit/transparent-zen#linux) 1. Open Zen Browser and go to `about:config`. Ensure the installed version of Zen is above release **1.11.2b** 2. Make sure that `browser.tabs.allow_transparent_browser` and `zen.widget.linux.transparency` are set to **true** 3. For a blurred window with **KDE PLASMA**, install [kwin-effects-forceblur](https://github.com/taj-ny/kwin-effects-forceblur) through your system repository, or build from source - Follow steps shown on the project Github to enable blur, and add `zen` to the allowlist ##### TODO: Add other OS prerequisites [Permalink: TODO: Add other OS prerequisites](https://github.com/frostybiscuit/transparent-zen#todo-add-other-os-prerequisites) ## Installation [Permalink: Installation](https://github.com/frostybiscuit/transparent-zen#installation) To install Transparent Zen, you can either download it from the [Firefox Add-Ons Store](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/transparent-zen) or grab the latest release from github and manually install it through the Zen Browser settings. #### Dark Reader [Permalink: Dark Reader](https://github.com/frostybiscuit/transparent-zen#dark-reader) If you are using Dark Reader, ensure that it is disabled for the websites you want to view as transparent. ## Usage [Permalink: Usage](https://github.com/frostybiscuit/transparent-zen#usage) Once installed, Transparent Zen will automatically apply transparent styles to the supported websites listed above. No further configuration is required. #### Customization [Permalink: Customization](https://github.com/frostybiscuit/transparent-zen#customization) I highly recommend darkening the browser theme slightly by right-clicking the Zen Browser Toolbar and selecting **Change Theme Colors**. I'm personally using the hex code **#00000066** which darkens the browser background slightly while maintaining readability. But of course, feel free to play around to find the best color to your liking! ##### ⚠️ Since Zen Browser 1.8.1b you need to set `zen.theme.gradient.show-custom-colors` to **true** in `about:config` in order to be able to set a hex code for the theme color [Permalink: ⚠️ Since Zen Browser 1.8.1b you need to set zen.theme.gradient.show-custom-colors to true in about:config in order to be able to set a hex code for the theme color](https://github.com/frostybiscuit/transparent-zen#%EF%B8%8F-since-zen-browser-181b-you-need-to-set-zenthemegradientshow-custom-colors-to-true-in-aboutconfig-in-order-to-be-able-to-set-a-hex-code-for-the-theme-color) For a really clean experience I also recommend to remove the light background behind the "website rendering area" which was added with a recent update. To do that, just add this snippet to your `userChrome.css`: ``` :root:not([inDOMFullscreen="true"]):not([chromehidden~="location"]):not([chromehidden~="toolbar"]) { & #tabbrowser-tabbox #tabbrowser-tabpanels .browserSidebarContainer { & browser[transparent="true"] { background: none !important; } } } ``` If you're unsure where to find the `userChrome.css`, you can follow steps 1-5 here: [https://www.userchrome.org/how-create-userchrome-css.html](https://www.userchrome.org/how-create-userchrome-css.html) ## Contributing [Permalink: Contributing](https://github.com/frostybiscuit/transparent-zen#contributing) The main idea behind this project is for the community to contribute and add styles for their favorite websites, so that everyone can enjoy a fully transparent experience. If you would like to contribute to Transparent Zen, please follow these steps: 1. Fork the repository. 2. Create a new branch for your feature or bug fix. 3. Make your changes and commit them with a descriptive message. 4. Push your changes to your fork and create a pull request. ## License [Permalink: License](https://github.com/frostybiscuit/transparent-zen#license) This project is licensed under the MIT License. See the [LICENSE](https://github.com/frostybiscuit/transparent-zen/blob/master/LICENSE) file for more details. Enjoy your transparent browsing experience with Transparent Zen! ## About A browser extension specifically for Zen Browser to make websites transparent and match the browsers aesthetics ### Topics [css](https://github.com/topics/css "Topic: css") [theme](https://github.com/topics/theme "Topic: theme") [firefox](https://github.com/topics/firefox "Topic: firefox") [firefox-addon](https://github.com/topics/firefox-addon "Topic: firefox-addon") [styles](https://github.com/topics/styles "Topic: styles") [zen](https://github.com/topics/zen "Topic: zen") [transparency](https://github.com/topics/transparency "Topic: transparency") [transparent](https://github.com/topics/transparent "Topic: transparent") [zenbrowser](https://github.com/topics/zenbrowser "Topic: zenbrowser") ### Resources [Readme](https://github.com/frostybiscuit/transparent-zen#readme-ov-file) ### License [MIT license](https://github.com/frostybiscuit/transparent-zen#MIT-1-ov-file) ### Uh oh! web.title: Zen Zero - The minimal browser - Sameera Wijerathna
web.description: This is a guide to install the Zen Internet Addon and make your Zen browser transparent. Go into Zen Browser settings > Mods > Transparent Zen
web.markdown:
[❤️Show some love](https://www.buymeacoffee.com/sameerasw)

[Back to top](https://sameerasw.com/zen#)

# Zen Zero

## Zen Browser

The most minimal a browser can be. Zen Zero is a customized **Zen browser** which aim to keep away the distractions and focus on the web and make it look pretty with transparency.

Zen Zero - Zen Browser Transparency Setup - YouTube

Tap to unmute

[Zen Zero - Zen Browser Transparency Setup](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4Pjk_EsT9TE) [Sameera Wijerathna](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCJ_esHlbZgQ-iQHZGWE-fCQ)

![thumbnail-image](https://yt3.ggpht.com/bCHa228-kWzX82i9go-Bqh5IppDsy-P22Vglai1WqWuh8fAGlQtfACD1wkbua7yWvlS0Z2VB=s68-c-k-c0x00ffffff-no-rj)

Sameera Wijerathna219 subscribers

![Zen Browser Preview 1](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/preview-1.jpeg)

![Zen Browser Preview 2](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/preview-2.jpeg)

❮❯

This is a guide to install the **Zen Internet Addon** and make your Zen browser transparent. This is a work in progress and I will be adding more features and fixes as I go. If you have any issues, please reach out to me on any of my social media or community groups.

1. ## Install the Transparent Zen mod


Install the **[Transparent Zen mod _open\_in\_new_](https://zen-browser.app/mods/642854b5-88b4-4c40-b256-e035532109df/)** from the mod store.
    [![](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/zen-mod.jpeg)](https://zen-browser.app/mods/642854b5-88b4-4c40-b256-e035532109df/)
2. ## Go to mod settings


Once you have installed the mod, Go into Zen Browser settings> Mods > Transparent Zen and click on the **Settings** button.
![](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/zen-settings.jpeg)
3. ## Configure the mod settings


Then make sure the **Allow zen browser to be transparent** option is checked. (And if you are on Linux, you may see another toggle to be turned on as well). Enable other options if you like and then quit and **re-open the zen browser** to apply the changes.
![](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/mod-settings-config.jpeg)
4. ## Install Zen Internet Addon


Go to the firefox addons page and install the **[Zen Internet Addon _open\_in\_new_](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/zen-internet/)**.
    [![](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/zen-internet-addon.jpeg)](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/zen-internet/)
5. ## Sync and Fetch the latest themes


Open the addon pop-up and click on the **Refetch latest styles** button to fetch the initial styles and keep the auto update toggle on to keep them up to date as I make changes.
![](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/zen-internet-sync.jpeg)

Congratulations

Your browser is now transparent

At least I hope so

You can now enjoy the zen browser with a transparent background. You can also adjust various features provided for each website when you open the pop-up in that webpage.

## Troubleshooting

- At the moment, only some of the operating systems are known to support transparency. Here are some:

  - macOS
  - Windows 11 - native mica and acrylic
  - Windows 11 - With mods like **[mica for everyone _open\_in\_new_](https://github.com/MicaForEveryone/MicaForEveryone)** or **[DWM blur glass _open\_in\_new_](https://github.com/Maplespe/DWMBlurGlass)**
  - Windows 10 - With **[DWM blur glass _open\_in\_new_](https://github.com/Maplespe/DWMBlurGlass)**
  - Wayland on **[KDE with Kvin blur _open\_in\_new_](https://www.reddit.com/r/zen_browser/comments/1jd0ggm/comment/miemhvg/?utm_source=share&utm_medium=web3x&utm_name=web3xcss&utm_term=1&utm_content=share_button)**
  - Hyprland - instructions



    **Tested on version 1.14.8b flatpak version**



    **Step 1: Enable flags in about:config**



    - widget.transparent-windows
    - zen.theme.gradient.show-custom-colors
    - zen.theme.acrylic-elements (not sure)

**Step 2: Install Transparent Zen mod**(enable transparency features for linux as well)

**Step 3: Use Nebula configs**(Nebula.uc.js userChrome.css userContent.css Nebula/)

**Step 4: Configure Hyprland transparency**\- Set opacity to 0.9 (exact value doesn't matter much)
- If you are using a different OS, please let me know if it works or not.

- Check your power saving settings and make sure that’s off on Windows.
![Windows power saving settings](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/win-power-saving.jpg)
- Make sure your Windows color settings are not affecting transparency.
![Windows color settings](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/win-color.jpeg)
- Hard to read text? black text in dark mode? It's because that website is not in dark theme or doesn't have one at all. In that case, you can use the **[Darkreader addon _open\_in\_new_](https://darkreader.org/)** to fix this behavior.
![Darkreader](https://repository-images.githubusercontent.com/26682105/d6f7dbef-46e9-4fa0-8961-8b73d1b53167)

## Site specific features

Once you start browsing, Check the add-on pop-up on each website and you will see a list of features along with transparency.

![Zen Internet Addon](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/site-specific.jpeg)

## Theme request and issues

- Don’t see transparency for your favorite website? Request a theme.

- You can report bugs and theme issues from the addon as well.
![Zen Internet Addon](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/request-2.jpeg)
- Restricted websites and pages can not be themed and your requests will be closed.


## More Resources

[article\\
Legacy guide](https://sameerasw.notion.site/Zen-Transparency-1939c6099d4080468f02cf05ae50e827) [mail\\
Contact me](https://sameerasw.com/#contact) [news\\
Reddit](https://www.reddit.com/r/zen_browser)
